location.office_time_required: Open to travel to Asia, including traveling at times to our Hong Kong or Taiwan locations

level_signals: 8+ years of product management experience or equivalent
Executive communications and stakeholder management expected
Cross-functional leadership across hardware, software, and partnerships

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of product management experience or equivalent
Demonstrated success and strong execution track record in building and sustaining scalable technology products.

Great interpersonal skills and strong verbal and written communication. You can collaborate and thrive in productive, healthy debate done with mutual respect and shared goals. You can make complex concepts accessible with clear communication
Proven track record of successful stakeholder management including executive communications, handling escalations, and advocating for ongoing investment in your product.
Experience working on products with developer interfaces (App deployment, APIs, SDKs); hardware or hardware-related product experience a plus.
Technically adept systems thinker, ideally with a Computer Science or equivalent degree or experience working as a software engineer
Open to travel to Asia, including traveling at times to our Hong Kong or Taiwan locations.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience successfully building and launching 0-1 products
hardware or hardware-related product experience a plus.

scope.surface: Stripe Terminal device software platform — device OS, firmware, boot, updates, security, connectivity, accessories
scope.business_model: B2B platform — in-person payments infrastructure for businesses and platforms
primary_metrics_inferred: OKR achievement for Terminal OS Platform and Firmware teams
core_responsibilities: Set a motivating multi-year vision for device software in partnership with the Hardware team and then break it down into measurable, meaningful milestones for the Terminal OS Platform Team and the Firmware team
Partner with software and hardware engineers to deeply understand the technology that our devices rely on
Partner with cross-functional partners to research, document, and deeply understand what Terminal customers need
Build empathy with our end users and the differentiation they aim to deliver for their merchants
Deeply research and understand the competitive landscape and translate that understanding into differentiating features
Collaborate with the Partnerships team to develop a compelling roadmap for how we can extend Terminal growth via third-party hardware partnerships
Use metrics to inform your point of view and leverage analytics to measure success
Define product requirements, provide clear guidance on prioritization, and support decisions related to development sequencing

green_flags: Clear product ownership area with specific technical depth (device OS, firmware, AOSP)
Multi-year strategy ownership — senior scope with real influence
Well-known company with large-scale infrastructure impact
Nice-to-haves explicitly broken out in a separate section, reducing ambiguity
red_flags: Location/work arrangement not stated — unclear whether remote, hybrid, or onsite
Travel to Asia (Hong Kong/Taiwan) required — significant commitment not quantified
8+ years hard requirement with broad scope may indicate high bar or role already internally staffed

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 8+ years requirement, executive comms scope, and multi-year strategy ownership)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and team mission)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
ambiguities: Hardware product experience appears in both must_haves (as part of developer interface requirement sentence) and nice_to_haves (explicit 'a plus' language) — treated as a soft/nice-to-have based on 'a plus' qualifier
Location type is entirely unstated in the JD body — no mention of remote, hybrid, or onsite. Cities listed (Hong Kong, Taiwan) are travel destinations, not primary work locations.
The CS degree qualifier uses 'ideally' which could be soft, but it is nested within a 'We're looking for someone who has' required section — classified as hard with bridgeable='maybe'
